text: I really don't understand Amazon and their "fit" guidance telling me to order my "usual" size because they are "true to size". Come to think of it, I don't think I've ever seen anything NOT supposedly "true to size". Anyway, I usually wear a 2X-3X (5'1"/230 lbs), so I ordered a 3X. They run so small that I'm not even going to bother trying a 4X. It's so hard to find anything vaguely appropriate for athleticwear for plus size women, so this was pretty disappointing. I got into mountain biking two months ago and was hoping to find something appropriate for colder weather and just in general because I'm tired of wearing my hiking pants while biking.
Too confusing with most of the pieces cut in the same pattern. Hope the person I passed it on to,has better luck and doesn't hate me.

I have a 57' chevy golf cart and it's fun to dress up when i ride around in it.... I will wear this with white bobby socks and saddle shoes or white keds.  I have lots of fun accessories - probably will wear it with my white scarf tied around my pony tail and cat eye sunglasses.  It's really about fun.  About sizing... I am 5'8"... 38DDD and usually wear a 14/16 or 0x/1x.  I read other reviews and decided to size up to an 18.  I feel that was a good choice, especially in terms of length.  There is a left side zipper, however, I am easily able to pull it over my head without using the zipper at all. The arms are VERY comfortable, NOT tight at all.  The material is stiff and starchy - which is good. Helps it to keep it's shape. BONUS:  It is stretchy!  There is a wide tie-on belt included.  The collar stands up and is a little odd... but will work.  NO pockets. The top wraps across your chest but it is not a wrap around dress... I like that that it is very modest.  NO cleavage showing.  The dress falls well below my knees - to what I consider the perfect length for this type of dress... ALSO... I did buy and am wearing a full petticoat under the skirt. There are many to choose from, I chose white and the xl worked perfectly. This really doesn't look like a true 50's retro dress, but it's close and it cute.  The red is a very slightly orange red.. and the dots are bright white and large. (See pics I have included)  This is really a "costume" but it the quality of the material and shape is such I believe with proper care, it will last a very long time.  My inclination is to take it to the dry cleaners to be sure it is properly starched and pressed.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seems useful.  And I really hope that my review has been helpful to you.

Listen... for the money... these are exactly what you pay for.  They are thin... elastic wasted cotton blend shorts.  They are truly unisex... for boys and/or girls... they will likely last one season due to the lower general quality - but also because kids grow so fast!  So, overall, I am ok with them. It would be nice if they were a tad thicker - but again, you get what you pay for. I purchased this set for my 5 year old grandson.  In my opinion the sizing is good.  If you have a heavier kiddo - as they are elastic waisted - you may want to size up a size... my grandson is average size and they are not too snug or too loose.  Elastic waists fits a wider range of kids - so that's good. It also makes them easy to get on and off.  They live in Arizona - so shorts are part of survival there. I will admit a couple of the colors are little wild or.. not really ideal (the mustard yellow? or safety orange? Meh. It would be great if they gave a couple of navy or couple red instead of those!) But honestly, my youngest generally doesn't care.  The 8 year old is another case!  haha  I haven't washed them up yet.  Having said that, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.  Hope this info was helpful!

I am surprisingly disappointed in this swimsuit. Based on the way it looked on the model, I figured it would be flattering and athletic looking. However, this is extremely unflattering. Any tiny or large tummy bump or body flaw is accentuated. Also, the chest area has padding that rolls over onto itself even when putting on the suit. I personally do not like padding but if it is secured well, I can accept it. In addition, I had a lot of trouble getting this suit over my shoulders. I felt like I was putting on a straight jacket. Once I got it up, however, it was loose on the bottom and comfortable on top after I unrolled the back that curled up.  Another disappointment is that the front part of the suit is lined and this lining moves around and bunches up in spots. It is only secured on the outer perimeter of the bathing suit, so I have to constantly readjust.  The dark blue and black colors with the yellow trim is very attractive but this suit is designed more for an evenly proportioned svelte person or woman bodybuilder with zero fat.

I ordered two. Shipping is a little slow, but they arrived in good condition. First, these bags are huge.  I weighed them with my luggage hook and it said 9 pounds, just as advertised. Lots of neat features. The wheels are protected by little edges and reinforced corners, so they may last a bit longer when battered by the baggage handlers. There is a extender zip, but honestly they are so huge inside I can't imagine using it.  As I was unpacking them I discovered there is a handle on the bottom where the wheels are! What a brilliant idea.  This means you can grab this suitcase just about any way it lands. SUPER handy when you're chasing your bag around the carousel because it landed wheel side toward you. There was a bit of chemical smell when I opened them, but I just aired them out and it is greatly reduced. I might lightly mist them with some Febreeze. But only if the smell lingers. The zippers are very heavy duty and the main zips do have the lock loop.  Inside there is a flat, top zip area and two adjustable straps on the bottom main compartment with a small side, mesh zip bag that is attached. Pretty standard confirguration inside. There is a limited warranty for manufacturer defect only and NO airline or consumer abuse and/or normal wear issues. I did purchase the basic black because they were cheaper, but here's a tip:  I use permanent sharpie pens (gold, silver and copper - sold on Amazon for about $5.99 a set of three) and a stencil (also on Amazon and themes to fit everyone out there... at about $7.00 a sheet) to personalize my suitcases so they are quick and easy to spot on the baggage carousel.. even at a distance. Because they are uniquely decorated, no one ever confuses my bag for theirs. Win/Win. Tag inside one bag said "Designed in the USA, made in China"  Thought about docking one star for that part.... I travel a lot and will update if there are any wear or other issues. UPDATE 6/15/2014:  The chemical smell is a bit more than I'd like... doing the Febreeze and I think a day out in the sun.  Some might consider a "new car" type smell, however, I am sensitive to chemical smells in general.

UPDATE 8/26.... additional purchase......Years ago (about 15 years I think) I purchased my first pair (actually 2 pair) of Donald Pliner shoes/boots... they were ankle boots I found in a magazine while visiting Scottsdale Arizona.  Looked up where they could be found in Scottsdale at a little boutique and bought them in tan/brown and black/red!  I STILL have them.  And to this day when I wear them at least one person asks where I got them - last was a TSA agent at the airport.  :)  DP has a way with shoes in general.  Quality and style for certain. Regarding these slides... i purchased the size 9.  I think they are true to size. The slide tops are snug, but not pinching. The inner sole is cushioned, but not too much. These were a steal of a price and I could not resist.  They are simple slides with a little height - around an 1 1/2" - maybe a bit less -in case height matters to you... they will go great with capris, skirts, and jeans. The black is true black and they crystals are clear.  A nice sparkle, but not too flashy - in my opinion at least. In fact, I'm going back to look at the other color that was available... because when they shoe fits, buy it in every color!  I have included a few pics... but just so you know, I do NOT receive compensation of ANY kind for my reviews. All of my purchases are VERIFIED, full price, and completely independent purchases, as are my reviews.  100%. What were they thinking?  This FOSSIL WOMEN’S ‘MODERN COURIER’ QUARTZ STAINLESS STEEL WATCH (Color:Gold-Toned (Model: BQ3378)) is absolutely ginormous.  With its clumsy, heavy, metal watchband, it weighs 4.3 ounces; and at 1⅝ inches across, it’s slightly bigger than a U.S. silver dollar!  What woman would want to wear this on her wrist?  And what woman who actually needs a chronograph would want to wear this one on her wrist?  The analog watch has three dials with teensy tiny numerals--they show 30 minutes, 24 hours, and 60 seconds. The stopwatch numerals are too small and hard to read for serious use.  Also, the start/stop and reset buttons are fumbly, which isn’t good if you need accurate splits.  The watch box does NOT include operating instructions, and the Fossil web site you are directed to for the instructions does NOT have any for this Model BQ3378 chronograph.  Thanks to another reviewer here, who explained how the watch generally works, I was able to figure out how to use this chronograph.  Operating instructions: Press the top button (above the stem) to start and stop the stopwatch, and press the bottom button (below the stem) to reset the stopwatch.  If the stopwatch gets out of whack and needs a complete reset, you do that by pulling out the stem and pressing both buttons at once so that the pointers advance until they’re both back at the 12 o’clock position.  Despite the crystals around the watch face and the gold coloring, this watch doesn’t look at all like a woman’s watch.  It’s too big to look pretty on a woman’s wrist.  For the price (about $115 on Amazon), you can do far better by searching for smaller, decorative women’s watches, or for good, accurate, unisex chronographs.

I selected this wallet because I am a little concerned about the possibility of having someone discretely scan my credit cards. This wallet claims to have RFID shielding and it is a stylish tri-fold, but it comes up short in a few key areas. I have trouble recommending this wallet as a result.  In terms of styling and appearance, this is a nice men’s wallet, with plenty of pockets for credit cards and licenses. It is a tri-fold wallet so it fits in a smaller pocket but it is thicker. That’s neither praise nor criticism; it’s something to consider when shopping.  I found two problems with the way the wallet is made. First, there is a linear mark on either side of my wallet that suggests it was picked up and crushed with something like a strong pair of tongs or forceps. It’s very likely my specific wallet, not an issue that affects every wallet in assembly. But, it never should have made it out of the factory. The other issue is more serious and that is the way it is sewn around the edges. The fold-over edge of the leather is very narrow and sewn close to the edge so that the corner is already protruding a bit and catches easily. It is a matter of time before the stitching breaks, and when it does this wallet will quickly come apart.  Those things aside, I took my wallet to the office where we have RFID proximity badge readers. I put my employee badge in the wallet, walked up to the door and scanned. With the wallet about an inch away from the scanner, it immediately scanned my badge and the door opened. It was reproducible about 80% of the time. A friend in the office also has an RFID wallet and the same test with his wallet gave about a 5% scan success rate. That’s a very big difference and does not speak well of this wallet. Neither wallet allowed the scan to work when more than an inch or so from the scanner.  I really wanted to like this wallet, but I cannot recommend it. It’s inexpensive and it looks nice, but otherwise there are better choices in RFID wallets.

I find the sizing to be true.  Just be prepared for the amazing, youthful look you're going to achieve with this bra! Torrid, if you're listening.... a TRUE NUDE please!!  The black is great, the white is true white.  Lose the TOO pink back to the nude!! :-)

UPDATE BELOW - PLEASE read update for sizing info........I recently injured my knee (torn meniscus) and I needed some easy to pull on and off pants... these are AWESOME.  I am 5'7" usually wear a 14/16 or 0x/1x.  I purchased the xxl for the gray and black - I am going back for more and the navy but I will purchase the xl.  The xxl will work but are a bit loose on the top.  The pants have winning pockets (deep but not too deep and easy to get into), material is super soft and they are easy to pull on/off.  The are soft like pajama pants, but don't look like pajamas.  Totally appropriate to wear in public.  They are the perfect length for me, hitting the tops of my feet.  Machine washed and tumbled dried beautifully (on low).  If you need a comfy, easy to wear pant for reasons as mine or just because - I think these are worth the try in my opinion. UPDATE 10/29/22 - I actually went back and ordered the LARGE!  It stays up.  Especially when you have your cell phone in one of the pockets! But they are not tight at the waist - very comfortable at the waist.  I am not super tall (5'7"), and they reach just below the ankle on me, which is perfect for me - but some over 5'7" may find them short. They are super soft and I am very pleased at how easily they wash and dry (on low) - no pilling so far.  Lots of great color options.  Wider legs - so if you have wider legs, they are flattering - no clinging. I am still a fan.  I think if you're an apple or pear shape, you might try one size down.  If you are more hour glass (me) - I would recommend trying 2 sizes down- unless you like a very loose fit and don't carry your cell phone in your pocket. This vest could not be more wrong.  First, the vest itself is nice but way TOO SMALL. It is Asian sizing; so an XL fits like a large in women's even though it says it is a USA XL. That is untrue.  Second, it comes with a battery pack, cable, and wall plug. The cable supplied looks like a micro USB cable that is the larger USB A-Type connector on one end and the smaller Micro USB B on the other side. However, the battery pack and the wall plug are both the female ends that take a USB A-Type connector. There is no place to put the Micro USB B end of the cable. This means this battery pack cannot be charged since I do not have any cable that has USB A-Type on both ends. I think it needs a cable like [[ASIN:B07KJ96M2W this one]], but with such a shoddy product (see third problem below), I am not willing to buy that cable to try it.  Third, the battery pack came charged already with three bars (instead of a full charge of 4 bars) - see last photo. I decided to try it and plugged it into the vest. The cable plug is located on the inside vest pocket. Nevertheless, when I pressed the on/off button that is at the top of the vest, it did not work. The on/off button is supposed to turn red for high, white for medium, and blue for low heat.  The vest itself is nice if one can fit in it. It has two tiny outside pockets at the waist level and one inside pocket for the battery pack. It is lined with fleece on the inside and feels nice and is warm just by itself without a battery.  In its current condition, this is not worth anything to me because it neither fits right nor does the battery pack work with the vest and it cannot be charged. If the vest fit me without having to squeeze into it, I would say it is worth $20. If the vest fit and the battery pack worked, it might be worth $45. The battery pack is going in the garbage since it will not work with the vest even if I got the right cable. This is an utter disappointment. My recommendation is to run from this product.

I'd give these 10 stars if I could - it seems simple, but it's the best thing I've used for slip on access to tie on shoes.  I've tried super strong magnet clasps (SUPER hard to to adjust - need a second person while you're standing in shoes!).  Tried the corkscrew elastic laces (did NOT lace - just knotted up). And then I found these watching shark tank.  I did make a mistake and order my first set too long (NOTE: measure the laces you want to replace) - so I ordered 2 pairs of the right size.  I'm looking at ALL my lacing shoes to determine how many more I need and what colors.  Amazon is a little lacking in selection but you can buy them directly.  I honestly love these.  They are SUPER easy to adjust unlike all the others I have tried. It takes just a minute or two to hit the sweet spot on the adjustment - tightness to wear and the right amount of give to slip on - and then you're off!  It seems so easy that it couldn't possibly be good, but you've gotta try to believe it.  I only bought 1 pair because I had been so disappointed with others.  I just couldn't be happier.  It's so cool to slip on shoes and go!

It fits just right on me. The chest and arms fit well, there is no pulling or gapping in the buttons and there isn't any exposure of bra straps or the side of a bra in the arm hole area. The length is nice on me as well, it came down to about an inch above my knees.  The only cons about this is the fabric, it is pretty thin and a tiny bit on the sheer side. But as long as you aren't wearing contrasting underclothes it shouldn't be an issue, or stick a slip under it and it will be just fine. The print is a bit iffy, but it was not misrepresented by the picture, it does look just like the close up photo that shows the details, so that was my overlook by not checking the other pictures available.  The belt that comes with it is your usual run of the mill, cheapy belt. But if you don't have many belts and want to visually pull in your waist, then it is a nice addition.  Overall I think the price could come down a few bucks. It was $20 when I purchased it and I'm OK with paying that since I am really happy with the fit of it. I don't think I would buy more though unless I catch one of the other colors on sale. If it was around $10-$15, I would pick up another color or two.  UPDATE: 6/7/17  Part of me wants to knock a star off after I washed it, but I feel like that may be a mess up on my end. Especially for letting someone else do the laundry when there was a new item in it, not sure if it got washed on cold or warm. But it shrank. I technically can still wear it, it fits. But it really is too short for me to comfortably wear it out. It went from about an inch above my knee to like 3 inches, so it is a significant difference. Especially on me since I'm so tall. On someone more of an average height, this might not be a problem. Part of me wants to re-purchase in another color and make sure to handwash it or something since I do like the dress. But I don't want to drop more money on it if there is a chance of it shrinking again. Too bad I can't size up to account for shrinkage. So it's still a nice dress, but keep the shrinkage in mind and make absolutely sure to follow the care instructions.

UPDATED REVIEW BELOW The jewelry box = about 4 stars The musty, moldy STINK that permeates this entire box inside and out.... negative 5 stars... So 2 stars is very generous. Honestly, I REALLY wanted to love this box.  It has nice, handy drawers... they are softly lined and the dividers can be removed if you would like to use the drawers differently.  It looks nice.  The side flaps have nice pockets to hold longer necklaces in as you close the flaps.  The mirror works if you are putting the box on top of a taller dresser... I was going to set it on my really big bathroom counter, so the mirror was really not useful for me.  The top mirror flap is opened by lifting and then sort of held open with a thin ribbon. It does NOT lock - in case that matters you.  There is a handle for lifting and moving the box if you desire - make sure the side flaps are snapped shut before moving. I noticed that having the side flaps open helped the top lid stay open. Here is the bottom line... when I opened the box an OVERWHELMING musty, moldy odor nearly knocked me over.  I don't consider myself to be overly sensitive to odors, however, this odor lingered in my living room for days. Now, this is supposed to be "leather."  It does have a very, very faint smell of leather... but it is easily overtaken by the must and mold. :(  I am attempting several strategies to remove the smell... but if you put anything in it, it is going to envelop that awful odor.  First I opened it all up, removing drawers and dividers  and let it set in the sun for an entire day.  For the last 2 days I have been spraying it with a Febreeze like antibacterial spray 3 times a day.  I don't really think I am making headway. It is likely going to the closest donation center... OR... perhaps my husbands tool shop. Pretty fancy for nuts and bolts, but who cares if they stink?  I always update my reviews if new info would be useful.  In this case and at this time, I just cannot recommend it for purchase. UPDATE:  11/13/2016.... Ultimately I was not able to remove the overwhelming odor emitted by the box. I felt it was so bad that I couldn't really take it to a donation center either. Husband said "No thanks" for use in his shop...so to the dumpster it went.  I am disappointed.  However, I will say that while I cannot recommend this box, the seller immediately contacted me following my review.  They did refund my money in full, after first offering a replacement or $15.00 credit.  The seller did also honestly communicate with me that the source all of the colors (3 to choose from) from the same manufacturer (so could be an issue with all of their stock) and the sellers were very fast with responses.  While I still cannot recommend this particular product, I would say that the seller was very apologetic and actively sought a way to make me happy so the seller gets a higher rating that the product.  I did also suggest that they check remaining stock, not sure if they plan to do or the ability to do so as the case may be stored and shipped from a location out of their immediate position. Sometimes is obviously wrong though.  I will not likely update this review further as I no longer have this case.

BEFORE BUYING you should know:  1. They are in Thailand. If you have to return the item for any reason, it will cost YOU approximately $20. (Mine was $18.95 from Oregon) They (Koh Koh) will also ask you to declare the value of the return as $10.00 so they do not get charged tax for the item being returned. No where in the Amazon posting does it indicate these dresses are shipping from (and returned to) Thailand.  It does says "FREE SHIPPING WORLDWIDE"... but that doesn't tell you anything really.  I will say that it arrived relatively quickly.  Too bad it was the dress was such a terrible mess.  ** UPDATE**  9/25 After a long communication "battle" a full refund, including my shipping has been processed.  2.  I actually really like the design of this dress. However, when the dress arrived (I ordered the red and white), the red part of the print had bled into the white and in some areas there were big red sploches on the white area of the graphic print. The bleed so bad that what should have been crisp, clean lines of the graphic design where "blurred" over the entire dress. I can't believe they actually shipped it to me in this condition. I would have liked to have ordered other colors (the navy/white and the black/white), but I will not risk they will be color bled or worse. What if it is a cleaning issue?  Possibly a bad dye batch? Who knows? The dress I received should never have been sent out.  DRESS DESIGN INFO:  I will say that I found the sizing to be acurate per their provided info.  The material hangs well and SHOULD be machine wash/tumble or hang dry, although I am not able to atest to that as it was immediately returned. The dress material is very thin but not see thru. To be honest, since it was such a hot mess I did not really inspect the sewing quality. The dress (design and material hand) does not look like a million dollar dress, but it would work nicely for casual wear or casual evening wear in my opinion. I also believe the design of the dress is one that would be flattering on a wide range of women and it is nice they offer some larger sizes. The red is sort of a brick and the photo is fairly accurate as to the color, and the white was a little more ivory/pink, but that is probably due to the ink bleed of the red onto the white. I did not find the "V" neck too low for me, but it would be possible to wear a cami under for modesty.  I strive to provide honest and geniunely helpful reviews each time I do so. I hope it has been helpful to provide this info.

These CALVIN KLEIN WOMAN’S IRAH SNEAKERS (size 8 medium) don’t have tongues—they’re cut like slip-on sneakers, except that the part replacing the tongue is NOT elastic.  I don’t understand why the Velcro straps are even there, because the sneakers aren’t adjustable.  They either fit you, or they don’t.  I wear a US size 8 medium in most walking shoes, but I cannot get these sneakers onto my feet without a shoehorn and a struggle.  (Needing a shoehorn is a big negative in a slip-on!)  Even then, the sneakers are too small to even attempt walking in them.  I strongly suspect that the inelastic top would prove uncomfortable if I were to try to wear the sneakers all day.  These “size 8 medium” sneakers are very short, and very narrow.  I think that I would have to go up at least a full size, to a size 9, for them to fit.  The sneakers have a strong chemical odor when you open the box, though I’m sure the odor will disappear when the shoes are worn.

I am writing a GoBros seller review. But first, a little about the socks.  I love Sockwell socks.  I wear them all the time for work.  The only negative comment I have about the socks is that they don’t last very long.  My socks tend to get holes in the toes.  Albeit, I still buy and wear Sockwells. If you are on your feet all day, I would recommend you try these socks.  Now for the seller, GoBros.  I bought two pairs of Sockwell from Amazon.  One came from GoBros and the other from another seller.  The GoBros seller sold me a, not advertised, pair of used socks at full price.  Yes, these socks were completely used.  It would not surprise me if the person who packed them also took them off of her own feet.  It was quite disgusting to open a package and see a used pair of socks. So disappointing and frustrating to get a used pair of socks, then have to waste my time sending them back and writing a review.  BUYER BEWARE OF GoBros seller.  To end, I do highly recommend Sockwells.

Looks 10/10 but don't purchase unless you want sizing frustrations, painful blisters and permanent scars.

I put this in the washer and dryer hoping they would shrink, and smooth down. Which they didn’t. I’m 5’2, 128 lbs. I should have ordered a  Medium Not Large at least  they would have fit. I do have sweat pants in large that fit smoother ,nicer and a better material then these are. These do have two pockets on each side that is nice, but not flattering. The bottom of the legs have a nice knit cuff , but there big and hangy, And just long enough to come to the bottom of my ankle bone almost. The material is like a cheesy polyester. These will probably fit my husband!  That said since the Covid I’m home almost all of the time, and these pants I can wear around the house.

I actually purchased two different styles of these wallets... this one with a zipper and one with a snap closure.  I much prefer this one.  I bought them to carry my id and perhaps a couple of bills and a card or two when I am not wanting to carry my whole purse.  The color is bright pink - as shown in the ad.  I wanted to be able to see it clearly in my purse without too much effort and the bright color really helps I've found. It does say RFID.. but I really don't have a way to verify that so I cannot say that it is keeping my credit card chip info safe or not... I do remain hopeful on that front.  I like this one because it is easy to get into and the zipper closure definitely "seals" your contents.  The one with the snap is really a pain to get it to snap closed and once I thought it was closed and was not. Everything spilled out.  :(  Soooo, in review, I would recommend this style over the snap style.  While the snap style maybe looks at bit more chic... I am opting for function..  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.
